report_name: South Staffordshire Water Fined Nearly £1 Million by ICO for Failures Leading to Cl0p Ransomware Breach

threat_actors.name: Cl0p
threat_actors.description: The Cl0p ransomware group gained initial access to South Staffordshire Water's network via a phishing email in September 2020. They remained undetected for 20 months before exfiltrating 4.1 TB of customer and employee data in mid-2022.
threat_actors.goals: Data exfiltration and financial extortion through ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The initial intrusion occurred via an employee opening a malicious email attachment, which installed malware and gave the Cl0p attacker a foothold.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker compromised a domain administrator account to facilitate lateral movement and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exfiltrated 4.1 terabytes of customer and employee data, which was later published on the dark web.

malware.name: Cl0p Ransomware
malware.description: Malware associated with the Cl0p group, installed via a malicious email attachment, which enabled a 20-month dwell time and the eventual exfiltration of 4.1 TB of data.

vulnerabilities.name: Poor Vulnerability and Patch Management
vulnerabilities.description: The company had multiple fundamental security failings, including unpatched critical systems and the use of obsolete software like Windows Server 2003, which had not received security updates for years and provided easy targets for the attacker.

identities.name: South Staffordshire Water
identities.description: A UK water company fined nearly £1 million by the ICO for severe data protection violations that led to a major cyberattack by the Cl0p ransomware group.

indicators.name: High Volume Data Exfiltration Alert
indicators.pattern:
title: High Volume Data Exfiltration Alert
id: 2b9c1f8a-1e3d-4b7c-8a2d-9f6b3e1a4c5d
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects alerts from Data Loss Prevention (DLP) or Network Traffic Analysis (NTA) systems indicating a high volume of data being transferred outbound. This is based on the 4.1 TB data exfiltration event in the South Staffordshire Water breach.
references:
  - https://ico.org.uk/about-the-ico/media-centre/news-and-blogs/2026/05/fine-of-nearly-1m-issued-against-south-staffordshire-plc-and-south-staffordshire-water-plc-following-major-cyber-attack-and-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  product: generic
  service: dlp
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'High volume data transfer detected'
    - 'Data exfiltration threshold exceeded'
    - 'Unusual amount of data sent'
  condition: keywords
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, large-scale data transfers or cloud backups.
indicators.description: Detects alerts from Data Loss Prevention (DLP) or Network Traffic Analysis (NTA) systems indicating a high volume of data being transferred outbound, as seen in the 4.1 TB data exfiltration event.
